topic.what: Foundational TCM text establishing the Wei (Stomach) and Pi (Spleen) organ system framework for understanding gastric disease.
topic.how: Five-phase correspondence model mapping gastric dysfunction to stomach heat, qi stagnation, and cold invasion patterns diagnosed through pulse, tongue, and symptom analysis.
topic.why_matters: Provides the theoretical basis for all subsequent TCM gastritis treatment — every modern TCM formula and acupuncture protocol for gastritis traces its rationale to this text.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether the framework covers etiology, pathogenesis, diagnosis, and treatment principles
characteristics.value: Complete: etiology (six pathogenic factors), pathogenesis (organ imbalance), diagnosis (pulse/tongue), treatment principles (tonify/drain)
characteristics.vs_baseline: More systematically organized than Hippocratic or Ayurvedic gastric frameworks

not_for: Direct clinical application without TCM training

novelty: First systematic organ-pair (Wei/Pi) model for digestion in any medical tradition
Stomach heat concept anticipates gastric acid excess by 2000 years
Qi stagnation framework provides a functional (non-structural) disease model absent in Western medicine until the 20th century
open_problems: Composite authorship over centuries makes attribution impossible
Five-phase correspondences are not empirically testable
Wei/Pi organ concepts do not map to anatomical stomach/spleen
